steps.title: Parallel lines have equal slopes — read slope directly from standard form
steps.content:
For $ax + by = c$ the slope is $-a/b$, no algebra needed.
Line $j$: $6x - 7y = 14$ $\Rightarrow$ slope $= -6/(-7) = 6/7$.
Since line $k$ is parallel to line $j$, it has the same slope.

steps.title: State the slope of line k
steps.content:
The slope of line $k$ is $\dfrac{6}{7}$.
The answer is $\boxed{\dfrac{6}{7}}$.
